accurate.why: All claims align with the bill text and accurately reference the Stafford Act and FTC.

no_hallucinations.why: No fabricated claims; contextual references (Stafford Act, FTC) are correct.

states_purpose.why: Clearly identifies the bill's purpose to prohibit price gouging during disasters/emergencies.

changes_to_existing_law.why: Notes treatment as FTC Act unfair/deceptive practices and Stafford Act declarations.

exceptions_conditions.why: Accurately describes the supplier-cost, tariff, seasonal hotel, and rental exceptions.

effective_dates.why: Captures the 30-day and 180-day periods, inflation adjustment timing, and 2-year limitation.

major_provisions_covered.why: Covers prohibition, enforcement, state actions, private right of action, penalties, and fund.

objective_tone.why: Neutral and descriptive throughout with no editorializing.

concise.why: Tightly written with no padding or repetition.

correct_entities.why: Correctly names FTC, Stafford Act, CPI, and Bureau of Labor Statistics context.

correct_figures.why: 10%, 50%, 30/180 days, $25,000 caps, and 2-year limitation all stated correctly.
